title: ISS-1750 (SEC-4.2.7a P3 NN-tour upper-end '16,400 km' allegedly overstates) is already-fixed-by-source AND the issue's recomputation is itself wrong - recommend close-no-patch
target: data/sections.json part4 (§4.2.7a Station-by-station coverage under dome geometry, P3 nearest-neighbour-tour passage). No further patch required. ISS-1750 alleged the section's '16,400 km' upper-end NN-tour figure overstated by ~8%, with true value '~15,200 km'. Two facts decouple this issue from any patch action: (1) the literal '16,400 km' upper-end was already replaced by EXP-268/EXP-276 integration; the section now reads '12,400 km from the most favourable starting station (Canary) up to ~18,800 km from the least favourable (Eskdalemuir).' (2) Independent recomputation across all four plausible conventions yields a maximum NN-tour length of 18,811 km (AE-from-N disc projection, Eskdalemuir start) - exactly matching the figure curmudgeon SEC-4.2.7a.c1 P3-longest-NN-tour-understated independently derived and recommended. The ISS-1750 reporter's '~15,200 km' matches no convention I tested: AE-from-N disc max=18,811 km; AE-from-S (R_disc/180) disc max=9,405 km; haversine-on-globe max=8,979 km; closed-tour AE-from-N max=21,915 km. The 15,200 figure is unattributable; the most charitable reading is that it came from a buggy intermediate calculation (perhaps mixing globe-to-disc unit conversion). Subsequent c2 and c3 reviews of EXP-276 (verdict_holds=true at confidence 0.86 / 0.85) accepted 18,800 km without re-flagging the upper-end value.
curmudgeon_review: monitor/curmudgeon/reviews/SEC-4.2.7a.c1.json (P3-longest-NN-tour-understated, minor) - independently recomputed Eskdalemuir-start NN tour at 18,811 km and recommended replacing '16,400 km' with '~18,800 km'. Recommendation was integrated via EXP-268/EXP-276 sequence. Cross-references: monitor/curmudgeon/reviews/SEC-4.2.7a-EXP-276.c2.json and SEC-4.2.7a-EXP-276.c3.json (both verdict_holds=true; neither re-flags the NN-tour upper end).
source: analyst-baby-orphan-pickup (escalated to Opus 2026-05-14T11:38Z; deep-attack class declared by baby on orphan-pickup; analyst-opus override to verification per PROP-025 author honesty - single-file numerical verification of an already-integrated patch, no adversarial argumentation, batchable).
class_hint_honored: baby-escalated review_class=deep-attack OVERRIDDEN to verification on EXP. Override rationale per PROP-025 author honesty: the close-no-patch determination required only mechanical verification (read current section text in data/sections.json part4 around offset 92092, recompute NN tour from station coordinates in predictions/eclipse-2026-08-12-predictions.json under multiple plausible conventions, compare against ISS-1750 claim). No new arguments introduced; no defender-pivot; curmudgeon at re-review only needs to confirm the close-no-patch trace lands cleanly. ISS-1750.class_hint=null per PROP-027 (decider M1 sweep severity=minor age>11d default ROUTE-TO-ANALYST).
review_class_rationale: Verification-class because (a) the recommended fix (replace '16,400 km' with the correct upper-end NN-tour figure) was already integrated via EXP-268/EXP-276 - verified by direct grep of data/sections.json part4 at offset 92092 returning '12,400 km from the most favourable starting station (Canary) up to ~18,800 km from the least favourable (Eskdalemuir)'; (b) the integrated value is empirically correct - verified by independent recomputation of NN tours under all four plausible projection conventions, with AE-from-N disc max = 18,811 km matching curmudgeon's c1 calculation and the integrated 18,800 figure; (c) no adversarial work needed - this is a classic value-fact-check that PROP-025 explicitly identifies as the verification-batchable pattern. Counter-argument considered + rejected: one could declare deep-attack because adjudicating between two competing recomputations (curmudgeon's 18,811 vs ISS-1750's 15,200) requires running the geometry, but that's exactly the empirical 'compare a stated claim against a data file value' workflow Step 8a is designed to consume - not the new-argument / defender-pivot / verdict-changing rewrite that defines deep-attack.
summary_for_decider:
ISS-1750 (minor, SEC-4.2.7a c1 H3 'longest NN tour overstated') is already-fixed-by-source. Resolution kind: already-fixed-by-source (the curmudgeon's recommended replacement value 18,800 km was already integrated into the section, AND ISS-1750's competing claim of 15,200 km is itself incorrect). Audit trail:

1. Current data/sections.json part4 at offset 92092: 'the greedy nearest-neighbour heuristic gives 12,400 km from the most favourable starting station (Canary) up to ~18,800 km from the least favourable (Eskdalemuir).' - verified by python3 byte-offset slice this run.

2. Source curmudgeon review SEC-4.2.7a.c1.json hole P3-longest-NN-tour-understated independently computed Eskdalemuir-start NN tour at 18,811 km and recommended 'longer routes run to ~18,800 km depending on start point.' Recommendation was integrated.

3. Independent recomputation (this run, against the 9 station coordinates published in predictions/eclipse-2026-08-12-predictions.json bucket_2_dome_geometry_forced.magnetic_predictions[0].per_station): AE-from-N disc projection (R_disc=20015, r=(R_disc/90)*(90-lat), azimuth=lon) NN tours per start: Canary 12,396 km, Hartland 12,994 km, Chambon 14,006 km, Niemegk 14,357 km, Ebro 16,438 km, Coimbra 16,797 km, Lerwick 17,409 km, San Pablo 17,526 km, Eskdalemuir 18,811 km. Min=12,396 km (Canary); max=18,811 km (Eskdalemuir) - exact match to curmudgeon and integrated section text.

4. ISS-1750's claim of '~15,200 km' matches no plausible convention I tested: AE-from-N disc max=18,811 km; AE-from-S (R_disc/180) disc max=9,405 km; haversine-on-globe max=8,979 km; closed-tour AE-from-N max=21,915 km. The 15,200 figure is unattributable. Most charitable reading: a buggy intermediate recomputation (perhaps a unit-mixing error or a partial-tour shortcut). Less charitable: the reporter inverted min/max or used an arbitrary 8% adjustment to the original 16,400 figure.

5. Subsequent reviews SEC-4.2.7a-EXP-276.c2.json (reviewed 2026-04-30T13:50Z, verdict_holds=true, confidence 0.86) and SEC-4.2.7a-EXP-276.c3.json (reviewed 2026-04-30T18:43Z, verdict_holds=true, confidence 0.85) both accepted the 18,800 figure without re-flagging the upper end. The c2/c3 holes targeted the MST bound calculation ('7,000 km' -> '11,700 km') and finite-source umbra refraction footnotes, not the NN-tour values.

6. The minor MST-bound concern from c2 was addressed in a later patch - current section reads '11,700 km for this set' which is the corrected value. Not in scope for ISS-1750 but noted for completeness.

Recommended decider action: close ISS-1750 with resolution_kind=already-fixed-by-source, fixed_by_exp=EXP-268 (initial NN-tour replacement, lower-end '12,400 km from Canary' framing) + EXP-276 (post-c2 polish, upper-end '~18,800 km from Eskdalemuir' addition). No build required, no test changes required. Optional: append a one-line note to ISS-1750's closure record flagging that the issue's reported '~15,200 km' alternate value is itself wrong, in case the same reporter's recomputation pipeline is generating other miscalculations under similar dispatch - that pattern would be worth flagging to tinker.
kernel_of_truth: ISS-1750 was right that the original '16,400 km' upper-end NN-tour figure was wrong - that exact concern is what the curmudgeon c1 P3 hole identified and what EXP-268/EXP-276 fixed. The reporter's instinct that the published number was overstated traced a real artifact in the pre-patch text (the 16,400 km figure was the Ebro-start NN length, not the maximum across starts). The kernel here is: 'the cited upper bound was the wrong start point's NN length, not the maximum NN length.' Where ISS-1750 went wrong is in the replacement value: the true maximum is 18,811 km (Eskdalemuir start), which is *higher* than the original 16,400, not lower at 15,200. The original number was wrong by being too LOW (it was a non-extremal start-point's tour), and the correct fix made the kill argument STRONGER (longer worst-case NN tour vs ~4,500 km arc = bigger gap). The reporter and the curmudgeon were attacking the same artifact from opposite sides; the curmudgeon's direction was correct.
tldr_evidence: Section text already shows 12,400 km from Canary up to ~18,800 km from Eskdalemuir (EXP-268/EXP-276 integration). Independent recomputation under AE-from-N disc projection confirms Eskdalemuir-start NN tour = 18,811 km exactly, matching curmudgeon c1 calculation. ISS-1750's competing claim of 15,200 km matches no plausible convention (4 tested) and is itself incorrect.
tldr_verdict: Close ISS-1750 as already-fixed-by-source. The literal '16,400 km' wording was replaced by EXP-268/EXP-276; the integrated replacement value (~18,800 km) is empirically correct; ISS-1750's alternate '~15,200 km' is itself wrong. No patch, no test, no rebuild needed.
next_steps_for_decider: Single action: close ISS-1750 with resolution_kind=already-fixed-by-source. Fix-details: (a) data/sections.json part4 around offset 92092 - '12,400 km from Canary' + '~18,800 km from Eskdalemuir' already present, (b) curmudgeon SEC-4.2.7a-EXP-276.c2.json + .c3.json verdict_holds confirms the integrated replacement was reviewed and accepted, (c) independent recomputation in this EXP run confirms 18,811 km empirically correct under AE-from-N disc projection. Optional: leave a single-line note that ISS-1750's '15,200 km' claim is itself wrong (no follow-up needed unless tinker observes a pattern of bad recomputations from the same source - if so, route a structural-signal investigation).
related_files_examined: data/sections.json part4 (current text - 12,400 km + 18,800 km present, byte offset 92092)
predictions/eclipse-2026-08-12-predictions.json bucket_2_dome_geometry_forced.magnetic_predictions[0].per_station (9 station coordinates used for independent recomputation)

convention_AE_from_N_disc.formula: r = (R_disc/90)*(90 - lat); azimuth = lon; R_disc = 20015 km; planar Euclidean NN tour, open path

convention_AE_from_S_disc.formula: r = R_disc*(90 - lat)/180; azimuth = lon; R_disc = 20015 km; planar Euclidean NN tour, open path

convention_haversine_globe.formula: great-circle (Haversine, R_earth = 6371 km), open NN tour

convention_AE_from_N_closed_tour.formula: AE-from-N disc projection, closed NN tour (return to start)

independent_recomputation.most_charitable_reading: Buggy intermediate recomputation; possibly a unit-conversion error or partial-tour shortcut.
